name: Commercial Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured regional bank serving Kentucky, Tennessee, and North Carolina with personal and business banking solutions. Nasdaq-traded institution offering checking, savings, credit cards, and lending services.
description_long: Commercial Bank is a regional financial institution operating across Kentucky, Tennessee, and North Carolina. The company is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, indicating federal deposit protection for customer accounts. Recently, Commercial Bank became traded on the Nasdaq Capital Market, reflecting its growth as a publicly-listed regional bank serving both consumer and business markets. The bank maintains a leadership structure with a board of directors and executive management team focused on regional market presence. Commercial Bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking products and services including personal and business checking and savings accounts, credit cards (VISA), mobile banking, and various lending products. The institution provides personal banking services, business banking solutions, credit card offerings, loan payment processing, online security features, and mobile app functionality for account management. Their lending services appear to include commercial lending, mortgage services, and government lending based on the executive roles listed. The bank also emphasizes deposit products and has dedicated deposit specialists available. The company differentiates itself through regional focus across three states with dedicated market presidents for different regions (Northern, Central, Eastern, and Southern). Their mission statement—"To create positive experiences for every customer, every day"—is prominently featured. Core values emphasize being knowledgeable, responsive, reliable, friendly, and team-oriented. The mobile app emphasizes convenience with features like remote check deposit, bill payment, fund transfers, and spending insights. The organization has established a governance structure with specific executives managing credit, risk, operations, and human resources. Commercial Bank functions as a traditional regional bank with standard banking infrastructure and regulatory compliance. The main limitation is geographic restriction to three states, which may not serve customers in other regions. Website content does not provide specific information about fee structures, interest rates, account minimums, credit card rewards, or competitive positioning versus other regional or national banks. The company's recent Nasdaq listing suggests institutional focus, though consumer-facing products remain available. For consumers outside KY, TN, and NC, or those seeking specialized financial services beyond traditional banking, this institution would not be accessible.
services: Mobile banking app with check deposit and bill pay
Online account management and fund transfers
Account login and enrollment for personal banking
Account login and enrollment for business banking
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit backing from U.S. Government
Nasdaq-traded public company indicating regulatory oversight and financial stability
Mobile app with remote check deposit, bill pay, fund transfer, and spending insights
Dedicated regional market presidents for personalized service across three states
Comprehensive business banking solutions with specialized commercial lending officers
Government lending programs with dedicated executive management
Dual personal and business account enrollment with separate login systems
Multi-region presence with branch locations across Kentucky, Tennessee, and North Carolina
cons: Geographic limitation to only three states (KY, TN, NC) restricts customer access
Website provides no information about account fees, interest rates, or minimum balances
No details published regarding credit card APR, rewards programs, or terms
Limited product innovation details beyond standard banking and mobile access
No published information comparing rates or terms to competitor institutions
best_for: Residents of Kentucky, Tennessee, or North Carolina seeking regional bank relationships
Small business owners needing commercial lending and business checking services
Consumers prioritizing FDIC insurance and established institutional stability
Customers wanting integrated personal and business banking at one institution
diagnosis: Commercial Bank is best for residents of Kentucky, Tennessee, or North Carolina seeking traditional banking services from a stable, FDIC-insured, publicly-traded regional institution. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to these three states—customers outside this region cannot access the bank's services, and the website lacks competitive rate and fee information needed to compare against other regional or national options.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: FDIC-insured regional bank serving Kentucky, Tennessee, and North Carolina with personal and business banking and lending services.
